bead.title: align efficacy filter controls with accessible labels
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The efficacy page filter test is timing out before it can exercise the URL-binding logic. `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/efficacy.spec.ts:226-240` looks for a combobox named `powerClass`, but the page renders a labeled select whose accessible name is `Power class` in `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/efficacy/+page.svelte:250-330`. The spec therefore never reaches the filter interactions it is supposed to verify.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/efficacy.spec.ts:226-240` targets the wrong accessible name for the power-class control.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/efficacy/+page.svelte:250-330` renders the actual filter form with `Power class`, `Label`, and `Spec ID` controls.

PROPOSED FIX
Align the Playwright selector and the rendered control contract so the efficacy filter test can reach the existing select/input widgets and verify URL encoding. Keep the change limited to the efficacy page and its e2e spec; do not alter the overall efficacy aggregation or comparison flow.

NON-SCOPE
- Efficacy aggregation data shape.
- Comparison dispatch behavior.
- Other project pages or screenshots.

ACCEPTANCE
1. `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/efficacy.spec.ts` US-096.b passes against the current efficacy page.
2. `cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun run test:e2e e2e/efficacy.spec.ts --grep "US-096.b"` passes.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
